source_policy: Official Microsoft Learn and Microsoft Support pages first. Prefer Excel Power Pivot-compatible DAX patterns unless the user explicitly targets Power BI.

entries.title: DAX overview
entries.use_when: Use for explaining what DAX is and how formulas behave in tabular models.

entries.title: DAX function reference
entries.use_when: Use as the first stop when a DAX function's exact page or syntax is unknown.

entries.title: CALCULATE function
entries.use_when: Use for modified filter context, context transition, and measure filter logic.

entries.title: ALL function
entries.use_when: Use for Excel-compatible total denominators and clearing filters in measures.

entries.title: ALLEXCEPT function
entries.use_when: Use when a measure must remove most filters but preserve selected grouping columns.

entries.title: FILTER function
entries.use_when: Use for table filter expressions passed into CALCULATE or iterators.

entries.title: DIVIDE function
entries.use_when: Use for safe ratios where denominators can be zero or blank.

entries.title: SUMX function
entries.use_when: Use for row-by-row weighted calculations over a table expression.

entries.title: RELATED function
entries.use_when: Use when a calculated column needs values from a related table through an existing relationship.

entries.title: SELECTEDVALUE function
entries.use_when: Use for single selected values; verify Excel version support and use HASONEVALUE/VALUES fallback when needed.

entries.title: Power Pivot in Excel
entries.online_query: site:support.microsoft.com/en-us/office Power Pivot Excel Data Model
entries.use_when: Use for Excel-hosted Power Pivot capabilities and customer-facing descriptions.
